title: Problems
summary: This page contains end-of-chapter problems from Chapter 1 related to signals and systems, including figures for problems P1.1-12, P1.2-1, P1.2-2, and P1.2-3. Problems involve plotting signals, analyzing time inversion, time shifting, time compression, and time expansion effects on signal energy. A specific problem asks students to sketch and analyze the signal x(t) = 2^(-t)u(t).

figures.caption: A collection of five continuous-time signals x(t), x₁(t), x₂(t), x₃(t), x₄(t), and x₅(t) with various piecewise-linear waveforms used for signal transformation exercises.
